summary: Planned Phase 6 (Uninstall and Upgrade) of Constraint Sharing: 6 tasks covering removeContributions core function, uninstall-constraints CLI command, upgrade detection in install-constraints, and round-trip verification.
pending: Task 2: Export removeContributions from core sharing index
Task 4: Register uninstall-constraints in CLI entry point
Task 5: Add upgrade detection to install-constraints (TDD)
concerns: removeContributions does not handle edge case where config sections were manually edited after install -- relies on key-based matching, not value comparison
Upgrade path uses synchronous uninstall+install in one function call -- if write fails between steps, config could be in partial state
decisions: removeContributions is a pure function in core (no I/O) -- CLI handles read/write orchestration
Upgrade is implemented as inline detection in install-constraints (remove old contributions before merge) rather than a separate upgrade-constraints command
Empty module entries are cleaned up (deleted) when all categories are removed
Uninstall with null/empty contributions succeeds but removes nothing from config
